title: Analects
author: Confucius
content: “By three methods we may learn wisdom: First, by reflection, which is noblest; Second, by imitation, which is easiest; and third by experience, which is the bitterest.”
action: Context: A breakdown of the cost and efficacy of different learning modalities.

title: Bhagavad Gita
author: Unknown (Attributed to Vyasa)
content: “You have a right to perform your prescribed duty, but you are not entitled to the fruits of action.”
action: Context: The decoupling of effort from outcome to maintain long-term operational sustainability.

title: The Epic of Gilgamesh
author: Unknown
content: “For where is the man who can clamber to heaven? Only the gods live forever under the sun. As for mankind, numbered are their days; whatever they achieve is but wind.”
action: Context: The confrontation with systemic mortality and the limits of legacy.

title: The Art of Worldly Wisdom
author: Baltasar Gracián
content: “Do not show your cards. The knowing never reveal their entire hand, but play it by degrees.”
action: Context: The strategic utility of information asymmetry in interpersonal dynamics.

title: The Prophet
author: Kahlil Gibran
content: “Your children are not your children. They are the sons and daughters of Life's longing for itself.”
action: Context: The recognition of independent agency in those we help cultivate.
